question_text: A blue filter is placed over a green light. A student observes that almost no light passes through. Which of the following best explains this observation?

options.label: A
options.text: The blue filter reflects all green light away

options.label: B
options.text: The blue filter absorbs the green light because green photons have different energy/wavelength than blue light that the filter allows through

options.label: C
options.text: Green and blue light cannot travel through the same space

options.label: D
options.text: The filter changes the wavelength of green light into blue light

explanation: Filters work by absorbing light of certain colours (wavelengths) and allowing only their own colour to pass through. A blue filter absorbs most wavelengths of light except blue, so when green light hits it, almost all the green photons are absorbed because they don't match the filter's transmission wavelength. The filter doesn't change wavelengths or reflect—it absorbs unwanted colours.
